design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot mecha suits through mission-based levels, switching between 6 distinct suit types with unique mechanics, collecting power-ups, and managing suit damage while ejecting to accomplish objectives as barefoot Stone
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of suit health and power-ups combined with tactical suit-switching during combat and navigation; tension between heavy armor combat and fragile unarmored scouting
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Commanding versatile robotic assault suits to overthrow an authoritarian military force; tactical freedom to approach problems with different mecha loadouts
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses to use traditional GUI—all information (damage, health, ammunition state) is shown through visual representation only. Demands players switch between mecha and vulnerable human form, creating asymmetrical gameplay. No password saves; progression tied to limited continues. Mission-based exploration over open level design

difficulty.lives_system: Limited continues system; players must complete game within continues budget before game over
difficulty.continues_system: Limited number of continues available per playthrough
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpoint structure; no mid-mission saves without passwords (which are not provided by default)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can adjust control settings and disable cutscenes before playthrough; can choose to eject from suits strategically to access smaller spaces and scout, trading combat capability for tactical positioning

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible power-ups for suit primary guns that alter shot patterns; backpack-mounted weapons (rocket launcher, grenade launcher, mine layer, gravity inverter); health restoration pickups. Power-ups can be disabled if suit takes significant damage
progression.unlock_mechanism: Mission-based progression through nine campaign missions; certain areas require Stone (unarmored) to eject and activate switches to open doors, forcing suit type transitions
replayability_hooks: Two-player split-screen deathmatch mode independent of campaign
Six distinct suit types with different mechanics encourage varied playstyle on replays
Optional hidden '?????' mode on title screen (method undocumented)
Adjustable difficulty settings and control customization at options menu

world_building_techniques: Year 2102 sci-fi setting with dictator Venkar Amon and Dark Axis military occupation
Progressive character arc: Stone promoted from lieutenant to captain after capturing asteroid base
Mission briefings that establish objectives (rescue agent Marissa, capture supply ship, defend main ship from counterattack)
Mecha anime influences evident in worldbuilding and suit design
Cutscenes can be disabled by player preference

faction_indicators: Metal Warriors (player faction): freedom-fighting group defending United Earth Government
Dark Axis (antagonist faction): military dictatorship led by Venkar Amon waging three-year war
United Earth Government: civilian authority under siege
danger_signals: Three-year ongoing military occupation of Earth
Systematic targeting of resistance assets (main ship attacked after early victories)
Armed enemy soldiers can steal player's mech suit during unarmored segments
environmental_storytelling: Progressive battle damage visible on mech suits shows cumulative combat stress
Space-based initial missions (Axis 5 station rescue, asteroid base capture) shift to Earth defense
Supply ships and resource acquisition missions indicate logistical warfare
Suit selection at level start reflects mission objectives (different suit types for different tactical challenges)
